unexpected_finds.title: Substring-only collision match on '*140*' filter from unrelated INDAG submission folder
unexpected_finds.description: STEP 0.2 supersession-gate scan with `Get-ChildItem -Recurse -Directory -Path sessions -Filter '*140*'` returned one match: `sessions/2026-04-25/263513140-INDAG-SUBMISSION`. This is an INDAG submission identifier whose decimal representation contains the substring '140'; it is NOT a slot-140 fire collision. Refined task-ID-shaped filters (`*-140` and `T2-EXECUTOR-POST-LEAN-REALITY-OUTLOOK*`) returned 0 results, confirming no parallel-CLI slot-140 collision. Judgment call: substring false-positive; proceeded with fire. Filed for awareness as a known false-positive pattern for future supersession-gate checks (numeric task-ID substrings under TASK_ID-shaped folder filters).

unexpected_finds.title: Aggregate project-side .lean sorry count concentrates in 2 of 5 surveyed files
unexpected_finds.description: Sorry-count survey at fire time: WallisFamily.lean=0, Thm66_ApparentSingularity.lean=2, proof_targets.lean=1, CardEvenOfInvolution.lean=0, TmpCheck.lean=0. Aggregate=3. Distribution is sparser than the iter13-build-error-log filename hints ('iter13' = 13 fix-passes worth of build failures); load is concentrated in Thm66_ApparentSingularity.lean (2 sorries; topical to M10 closure target Theorem 6.6) and proof_targets.lean (1 sorry; sandbox / target-statement file). Note: tactic-block context not classified by the survey; raw `\bsorry\b` regex count includes any literal sorry token. If operator picks BUNDLED-DEFERRED-NOTE branch (slot 141 Branch B), the documented commitment paragraph should cite both the iter-count metric (build_errors_iter{N}.log) and this aggregate-sorry-count metric so the commitment-by-date / report-status-by-date framing has two independent progress signals.
